output.answer:
## Current Status of Parking Revenue Recovery

Based on the memos, the parking revenue recovery effort is **actively generating results** since the City of Reno reinstated access to Nevada DMV registered owner data on June 20, 2025. The cumulative collections through February 28, 2026 are:

| Period | Tickets Paid | Revenue |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| June 20 – Dec 31, 2025 | 15,655 | $530,534.89 |
| January 2026 | 1,295 | $48,746.21 |
| February 2026 | 1,206 | $54,058.75 |
| **Total** | **18,156** | **$633,339.85** |

The program has also recently **gained access to California DMV data**, expanding the ability to pursue out-of-state violators.

---

## The Single Biggest Blocker

The **Nevada DMV holds** — which would automatically flag unpaid citations when a vehicle owner renews their registration, creating a powerful enforcement lever — **remain unimplemented**. The software vendor (AIMS) has completed its preparation, but the effort is stalled waiting on the **Nevada Department of Public Safety to process the setup request at the state level**, with no resolution across multiple reporting periods.
